mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A breach of the 4.5% unemployment rate threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which signals immediate economic momentum and triggers positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative or flat 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate earnings potential and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces near-term profit expectations and drives negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during contractionary phases.
